bullets.text: U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran enter second week; Iran vows no surrender, raising risk of prolonged conflict and regional spillovers.
Early Iran war campaign described as punishing but lacking a coherent endgame, increasing odds of mission creep and potential “boots on the ground” debates.
U.S. announces $20B oil-tanker reinsurance during Iran war, effectively socializing maritime risk to stabilize energy shipping and insurance markets.
Iran’s internet blackout extends into a second week, signaling tighter wartime information control and higher operational uncertainty for businesses and citizens.
Economic warning signs ahead of U.S. midterms intensify; Iran-war-driven price pressures could make affordability a central 2026 political issue.
Wall Street’s tough week driven by persistent inflation uncertainty, geopolitics, and shifting rate expectations—keeping volatility elevated across risk assets.
Middle-income buyers gained about $30,000 in housing purchasing power versus last year, but affordability remains constrained by prices and financing costs.
Candidates increasingly signal alignment with crypto and AI to attract donor cash, deepening tech-sector influence over U.S. campaign finance and policy priorities.
Senators push to bar federal officials from profiting via prediction markets, aiming to reduce conflicts-of-interest as forecasting platforms gain liquidity and attention.
China accelerates a U.S.-China space-power contest, investing in rockets and capabilities with strategic implications for defense, satellites, and high-tech supply chains.
OpenAI’s robotics lead quits after Pentagon deal, highlighting internal friction over defense work and increasing scrutiny of AI vendors’ government partnerships.
Researchers use AI to detect liquid-like ion flow signals in solid-state batteries, potentially accelerating breakthroughs in safer, higher-energy storage for EVs and grids.
